target.name: Bedrock Energy
target.founder: Joselyn Lai, Silviu Livescu, Joel Wishkovsky

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Bedrock Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Joselyn Lai, Silviu Livescu, and Joel Wishkovsky, having raised $15M+ to date. The Climate & Energy sector includes 1 public comparable (Lnza) and 8 private peers such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBolt Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tier density to support a reliable peer-median approach, limiting applicability of multiples-based triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation framework. Using the $15M total raised as a base value proxy and applying a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, this method implies a valuation of $30M. This single-method result forms the anchor point for the triangulated range.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M (low) to $34M (high), with a median of $30M. The narrow methodological foundation—reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than market-derived multiples—represents the primary valuation uncertainty. Resolution of this uncertainty would benefit from either accumulation of additional same-stage private comparables or demonstrated progress toward quantifiable operational or financial milestones that could anchor peer-based or DCF-derived approaches.
